api/spec/packages/aip/src/apps.purpose: TypeSpec definitions for the billing apps subsystem (Stripe, Sandbox, ExternalInvoicing). Defines installed-app models, customer data, checkout sessions, and app operations — all compiled to api/v3/openapi.yaml and Go server stubs.

patterns.name: AppBase generic spread
patterns.description: Every concrete app model spreads AppBase<AppType.X> instead of duplicating id/type/status/definition fields.

patterns.name: Discriminated union for polymorphic App
patterns.description: The top-level App union uses @discriminated with envelope:none and discriminatorPropertyName:type; every member must be a model that spreads AppBase so the discriminator field is always present.
patterns.example: @discriminated(#{ envelope: "none", discriminatorPropertyName: "type" }) union App { stripe: AppStripe, sandbox: AppSandbox, external_invoicing: AppExternalInvoicing }

patterns.name: Visibility-gated fields
patterns.description: Secret/write-only fields (e.g. secret_api_key) carry @visibility(Lifecycle.Create, Lifecycle.Update) only; read-only computed fields carry @visibility(Lifecycle.Read) only.

patterns.name: operations.tsp imports all HTTP decorators
patterns.description: Only operations.tsp imports @typespec/http, @typespec/rest, @typespec/openapi3 and declares 'using TypeSpec.Http'. Model files must not import HTTP decorators.
patterns.example: import "@typespec/http"; using TypeSpec.Http; namespace Apps; interface AppsOperations { @get list(...): ... }

patterns.name: index.tsp as barrel re-export
patterns.description: index.tsp imports all sibling .tsp files and nothing else; it is the entry point consumed by parent packages.

patterns.name: @friendlyName on every model/enum
patterns.description: Every exported model and enum carries @friendlyName with a BillingApp* prefix to control the generated SDK type name.

patterns.name: Shared.Resource spread for domain entities
patterns.description: Entities with identity (AppBase, not value types) spread Shared.Resource to inherit id, created_at, updated_at.

key_file_guides.role: Defines AppType enum, AppStatus enum, AppBase generic, App discriminated union, and AppReference. Core types consumed by all other files.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new app type here requires: new AppType member, new model that spreads AppBase, new union member in App, and a new file (e.g. newapp.tsp) imported in index.tsp.

key_file_guides.role: Full Stripe app model plus all Checkout Session and Customer Portal session types (~250 lines). Only secret fields use @secret.
key_file_guides.watch_for: @secret must be on create/update-only fields; do not add it to read-only fields. Use @maxLength on free-text fields.

key_file_guides.role: ExternalInvoicing app model with enable_draft_sync_hook and enable_issuing_sync_hook boolean flags controlling bi-directional sync pausing.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Sync hooks are plain booleans, not enums; adding a new hook state would require a discriminated union change.

key_file_guides.role: Declares AppsOperations interface with list and get operations only. All HTTP decoration lives here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Stripe-specific operations (createCheckoutSession, createCustomerPortalSession) are NOT in this file — they live elsewhere in the aip spec. Do not add customer-scoped ops here.

key_file_guides.role: AppCustomerData aggregate model grouping per-app customer linkage data (Stripe customer ID, external invoicing labels).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new app requires adding an optional field here with matching @visibility(Read, Create, Update).

anti_patterns: Adding HTTP decorators (@get, @post) in model files (app.tsp, stripe.tsp, etc.) — HTTP decoration belongs only in operations.tsp
Defining a new app model without spreading AppBase<AppType.X> — breaks discriminated union typing
Omitting @friendlyName on a new model — generates an uncontrolled SDK type name
Using @visibility(Lifecycle.Read) on secret_api_key-style fields — secrets must be write-only
Importing from outside the aip/src tree without using Shared.* or Common.* namespaces

decisions.decision: AppBase is a generic model rather than an interface
decisions.rationale: TypeSpec does not have interface inheritance for models; generic spread lets all app types share id/type/status/definition without copy-paste and keeps the discriminated union working.

decisions.decision: Stripe checkout/portal session types live in stripe.tsp not operations.tsp
decisions.rationale: Model definitions are kept separate from operation declarations so model files can be imported by other namespaces without pulling in HTTP routing concerns.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new billing app type (e.g. Adyen)

api/spec/packages/aip/src/billing.purpose: TypeSpec definitions for the billing profile subsystem: BillingProfile, BillingWorkflow (collection, invoicing, payment, tax settings), tax config, and totals. Compiled to the BillingProfiles* section of api/v3/openapi.yaml.

patterns.name: Workflow settings are nested sub-models
patterns.description: BillingWorkflow contains optional sub-models (collection, invoicing, payment, tax) rather than flat fields, allowing independent evolution of each settings group.
patterns.example: model BillingWorkflow { collection?: BillingWorkflowCollectionSettings; invoicing?: BillingWorkflowInvoicingSettings; ... }

patterns.name: Discriminated union for polymorphic settings
patterns.description: Payment settings and collection alignment use @discriminated(#{envelope:"none", discriminatorPropertyName:...}) unions; every member model must declare the discriminator field as a literal type.
patterns.example: @discriminated(#{ discriminatorPropertyName: "collection_method", envelope: "none" }) union BillingWorkflowPaymentSettings { charge_automatically: ..., send_invoice: ... }

patterns.name: @encode(DurationKnownEncoding.ISO8601) on duration fields
patterns.description: All duration/interval fields use @encode(DurationKnownEncoding.ISO8601) and carry an @example with ISO8601 string (e.g. 'P1D', 'PT1H').

patterns.name: Shared.Resource spread for profile entities
patterns.description: BillingProfile spreads Shared.Resource to inherit id/created_at/updated_at. Reference types (BillingProfileReference) contain only the id field.

patterns.name: Separate References vs full models for app linkage
patterns.description: BillingProfileAppReferences (used on create) holds AppReference objects; BillingProfileApps (read-only expansion) holds full App objects. This prevents over-posting.
patterns.example: model BillingProfileAppReferences { tax: Apps.AppReference; invoicing: Apps.AppReference; payment: Apps.AppReference; }

key_file_guides.role: Core BillingProfile and BillingWorkflow models. Largest file — defines all workflow sub-settings including alignment union and payment union.
key_file_guides.watch_for: DefaultBillingWorkflowCollectionAlignment is a const used as a default value; changing alignment types requires updating both the union and the const.

key_file_guides.role: TaxConfig, TaxBehavior, TaxConfigStripe, TaxConfigExternalInvoicing. Provider-specific tax config is additive — new providers add an optional field to TaxConfig.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Stripe tax code must match pattern ^txcd_\d{8}$; enforce @pattern on new tax code fields.

key_file_guides.role: BillingTotals read-only model with amount, taxes, charges, discounts, credits, total fields all using Shared.Numeric.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All fields are @visibility(Lifecycle.Read) — this is a computed output model, never a create/update input.

key_file_guides.role: BillingProfilesOperations CRUD interface using Shared.CreateRequest, Shared.UpsertRequest, Shared.CreateResponse, Shared.GetResponse, Shared.DeleteResponse.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Update uses @put + Shared.UpsertRequest (full replacement), not @patch. Diverging from this breaks SDK generated clients.

anti_patterns: Adding flat fields directly to BillingProfile instead of grouping them into a settings sub-model (breaks the workflow settings pattern)
Using @patch for update operations — the pattern here is @put with full UpsertRequest
Adding mutable fields to BillingTotals — totals are always read-only computed values
Omitting @encode(DurationKnownEncoding.ISO8601) on ISO 8601 duration string fields
Hardcoding app references as full App models in create/update bodies — use AppReference for write paths

decisions.decision: Payment settings modeled as discriminated union on collection_method
decisions.rationale: ChargeAutomatically and SendInvoice have disjoint extra fields (due_after only applies to SendInvoice); a union avoids optional fields that are meaningless in the wrong mode.
TypeSpec const (DefaultBillingWorkflowCollectionAlignment) lets the default value be a typed discriminated union member, not a bare string — keeping type safety on the default.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new workflow settings group (e.g. retry settings)

api/spec/packages/aip/src/common.purpose: Shared TypeSpec primitives for the v3 AIP spec: error response aliases, pagination query models, AIP-style filter types (String/ULID/DateTime/Boolean/Numeric/Labels), sort query, and label property references. All other namespaces import from here.

patterns.name: Error responses via @useRef to external YAML
patterns.description: All error models use @useRef pointing to common/definitions/errors.yaml; never define inline error schemas. The ErrorResponses alias groups the three universal errors (BadRequest, Unauthorized, Forbidden).

patterns.name: Filter types use @extension x-go-type for Go codegen
patterns.description: Every filter model/union carries @extension("x-go-type", "filters.FilterXxx") and @extension("x-go-type-import", #{path: "github.com/openmeterio/openmeter/api/v3/filters"}) so oapi-codegen emits the hand-written Go filter type instead of generating a new struct.

patterns.name: Pagination models split by strategy
patterns.description: CursorPaginationQuery and PagePaginationQuery are separate models; operations choose exactly one. Never mix cursor and page parameters in the same operation.

patterns.name: Sort via @useRef to aip_filters.yaml
patterns.description: SortQuery delegates to a YAML $ref — it is an empty TypeSpec model with @useRef. Do not expand sort logic inline in TypeSpec.

patterns.name: Filter unions support both shorthand and object form
patterns.description: StringFieldFilter, ULIDFieldFilter, DateTimeFieldFilter, LabelsFieldFilter are unions of (scalar | object-with-operators) so clients can pass filter[x]=value or filter[x][eq]=value.
patterns.example: union StringFieldFilter { string: string, object: { eq?: string, neq?: string, contains?: string, ... } }

key_file_guides.role: Canonical error response models and the ErrorResponses alias. Import Common.ErrorResponses at the end of every operation's return union.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ErrorResponses only covers 400/401/403. Ops that can 404 must add '| Common.NotFound' explicitly; ops that can 409 must add '| Common.Conflict'.

key_file_guides.role: All AIP filter types and SortQuery. New filter types must follow the union-of-(scalar|object) pattern and carry both x-go-type extensions.
key_file_guides.watch_for: StringFieldFilterExact is a model (not union) because shorthand scalar doesn't make sense for exact-only filters — don't accidentally make new exact-only types a union.

key_file_guides.role: CursorPaginationQuery, PagePaginationQuery, and their associated meta models. CursorMeta and PageMeta delegate to YAML $refs.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Cursor pagination uses page.after/before; page pagination uses page.number. Using the wrong query model breaks SDK pagination helpers.

key_file_guides.role: Labels and PublicLabels models delegating to konnect_properties.yaml via @useRef.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Labels are constrained to 1-63 char keys excluding reserved prefixes — constraints are in the YAML not in TypeSpec.

anti_patterns: Defining inline error response bodies instead of using @useRef to errors.yaml
Adding a new filter type without both x-go-type and x-go-type-import extensions — breaks Go codegen
Using Common.PagePaginationQuery on an operation that returns CursorPaginatedResponse (or vice versa)
Declaring non-empty bodies on Common error models — they are stubs that reference YAML
Mixing sort and filter logic inline in operations.tsp instead of referencing Common.SortQuery and Common.*FieldFilter

decisions.decision: Filter types carry x-go-type extensions pointing to hand-written Go filter structs
decisions.rationale: AIP filter semantics (eq/neq/contains/oeq/ocontains/exists) require parsing logic that is hard to generate; keeping the Go implementation in api/v3/filters/ and referencing it from TypeSpec avoids duplicating that logic in generated code.

decisions.decision: Error response models delegate to external YAML via @useRef
decisions.rationale: The YAML definitions are shared with the v1 spec and contain OpenAPI extensions; a TypeSpec-native redefinition would diverge and break shared client error handling.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new filter type for an enum field

api/spec/packages/aip/src/entitlements.purpose: TypeSpec definitions for the customer entitlement access query API (list feature access per customer). Minimal surface: EntitlementType enum, EntitlementAccessResult model, and a single GET list operation.

patterns.name: Response wrapper model for non-paginated arrays
patterns.description: ListCustomerEntitlementAccessResponse wraps data: EntitlementAccessResult[] in a named model rather than returning a bare array, matching the Shared.PagePaginatedResponse pattern for SDK consistency.

patterns.name: Explicit @statusCode on response models
patterns.description: Response models declare @Http.statusCode _: 200 inline rather than using a generic response wrapper, since this operation has no pagination metadata.

patterns.name: All result fields are @visibility(Lifecycle.Read)
patterns.description: EntitlementAccessResult is a pure read model; every field carries @visibility(Lifecycle.Read). No create/update visibility.

key_file_guides.role: Defines EntitlementType enum (Metered, Static, Boolean). Minimal — only add here if a new entitlement type is introduced.
key_file_guides.watch_for: The @friendlyName uses 'BillingEntitlementType' prefix — keep consistent with the Billing* prefix convention for this spec.

key_file_guides.role: CustomerEntitlementsOperations interface with a single list operation scoped to a customerId path param.
key_file_guides.watch_for: This operation is customer-scoped (@path customerId) — it does NOT use namespace or pagination params. Do not add Common.PagePaginationQuery here.

key_file_guides.role: EntitlementAccessResult model. The config field is optional and only populated for static entitlements.
key_file_guides.watch_for: has_access is always true for boolean/static types — ensure any new type documents this invariant in its JSDoc.

anti_patterns: Adding write operations (create/update/delete) to this folder — entitlement management is in the v1 spec, this folder is read-only access query only
Adding pagination to the list operation — the response intentionally returns all feature access in one call
Returning a bare array instead of the wrapper model with a 'data' key

decisions.rationale: The number of features per customer is bounded and small; pagination overhead would complicate client-side feature-gate checks.

api/spec/packages/aip/src/events.purpose: TypeSpec definitions for the metering event ingest and query API: CloudEvents-based MeteringEvent model, IngestedEvent with metadata, filter model, and operations for batch ingest and cursor-paginated listing.

patterns.name: @sharedRoute for content-type overloads
patterns.description: The ingest endpoint has three operation declarations (single CE, batch CE, JSON) all with the same @operationId and @sharedRoute so they compile to one OpenAPI operation with multiple requestBody content types.

patterns.name: Cursor pagination for event listing
patterns.description: Events use ...Common.CursorPaginationQuery (not PagePaginationQuery) and return Shared.CursorPaginatedResponse<IngestedEvent>. Filter and sort params are separate named query params.

patterns.name: Filter model uses Common.*FieldFilter types
patterns.description: ListEventsParamsFilter fields reference Common.StringFieldFilter, Common.ULIDFieldFilter, Common.DateTimeFieldFilter — never inline filter logic.
patterns.example: model ListEventsParamsFilter { id?: Common.StringFieldFilter; customer_id?: Common.ULIDFieldFilter; time?: Common.DateTimeFieldFilter; ... }

patterns.name: Ingest response is 202 Accepted with empty body
patterns.description: IngestEventsResponse declares @statusCode _: 202 and no @body, reflecting async processing semantics.

patterns.name: CloudEvents field constraints
patterns.description: MeteringEvent fields follow CloudEvents spec: id/source/type/subject have @minLength(1); specversion has a default of '1.0'; data is Record<unknown>.

key_file_guides.role: MeteringEvent (CloudEvents schema) and IngestedEvent (with ingested_at, stored_at, validation_errors). Core model file.
key_file_guides.watch_for: IngestedEventValidationError has code+message+attributes — new validation error codes in the Go layer must have corresponding documentation here.

key_file_guides.role: EventsOperations interface with list (cursor-paginated GET) and three @sharedRoute POST overloads for ingest. Filter uses @query(#{style:"deepObject", explode:true}).
key_file_guides.watch_for: All three ingest overloads share the same @operationId. Adding a fourth content-type overload must also use @sharedRoute and the same operationId.

anti_patterns: Adding pagination to the ingest endpoint — ingest is fire-and-forget 202, not paginated
Using PagePaginationQuery for event listing — events use cursor pagination (after/before) not page-number pagination
Defining filter operators inline in operations instead of using Common.*FieldFilter types
Removing @sharedRoute from any of the ingest overloads — this collapses three content-type variants to one in the OpenAPI output

decisions.decision: Three @sharedRoute ingest overloads instead of a single union body
decisions.rationale: OpenAPI requires distinct requestBody entries per content-type for correct SDK code generation; TypeSpec @sharedRoute is the mechanism to express this without duplicating the operation ID.
Event streams grow continuously and page-number pagination is unstable on append-only data; cursor-based (after/before) pagination gives stable traversal.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new filter field to event listing
